root.speaker: Old Woman
root.text: Oh. There you are. You took your time getting back. Sit, if you like — the bench remembers you better than I do.

met.speaker: Old Woman
met.text: Mm. Once. You were taller. Or shorter — it's hard to say with travellers. The light was different. You said something kind. I remember kind things.

said.speaker: Old Woman
said.text: I won't repeat it. It was meant only the once. — You were on your way down from the Wake. Tea was brewed. The morning was very long.

choices.label: I've never been to the Wake.

wake_dodge.speaker: Old Woman
wake_dodge.text: Of course not. Of course not. Forgive an old woman her loose threads. — Have you tried the seeds? Pim's roasted them well this morning.

book.speaker: Old Woman
book.text: Names. Names of every person who has danced in this square. The list is shorter than you'd think. I have to start a fresh page each morning, but the names stay the same.

book_no.speaker: Old Woman
book_no.text: No. The page belongs to the morning. It would not know your hand. — Sit a little longer. The lanterns will turn warmer in a moment; it's almost the second song.

place.speaker: Old Woman
place.text: A festival town. They will tell you what is being celebrated, and they will be wrong, and that's all right. — I remember a festival here, when my brother brought down a beam from the Forge for the stage. It was last week. It was four hundred years ago.

four_hundred.speaker: Old Woman
four_hundred.text: Mm. Don't dwell. The dancers are starting again. — There's a small glass on the table beside you, by the way. I won't be needing it. A traveller may.

bye.speaker: Old Woman
bye.text: Walk well. — Try the seeds before you go.

root.speaker: Innkeeper Brall
root.text: Welcome, welcome! New face! Or — hah! — newish. Doesn't matter! Today's the festival! Drinks half-price 'til the second drum!

what.speaker: Innkeeper Brall
what.text: What festival! What festival, the man asks! — ah, well. We celebrated something. A founding, maybe. Or a returning. Doesn't matter — the music's good and the seeds are hot!

yest.speaker: Innkeeper Brall
yest.text: Yesterday? Hah! Yesterday's a long word for a man behind a bar! We — we celebrated something. I'm sure we did. The banners aren't new. — Anyway! Try the dark ale, brewed this morning!

bye.speaker: Innkeeper Brall
bye.text: Walk well, walk well! Don't miss the second drum!

root.speaker: Reveller Mira
root.text: (spinning) Step in, step in! Don't think about your feet, the floor knows what to do! Whee — oh, hi! Hi! Bye!

root.speaker: Reveller Tas
root.text: Y'know what's beautiful, friend? Tomorrow. Tomorrow we'll do this again. Same lanterns. Same drums. Same — same everything.

same.speaker: Reveller Tas
same.text: Mm. Yeah. Same. Like — like the morning forgets and starts fresh, see? Fresh and the same. That's a kindness, that is. — Why am I crying? I'm not crying.

bye.speaker: Reveller Tas
bye.text: Hey — hey — see you tomorrow! Or, you know. Soon. In the next breath.

root.speaker: Reveller Ket
root.text: Oh — oh, look at all of it! Hey, you — hey — do you live here? Can I stay? Do they let people stay? I think I want to stay.

stay.speaker: Reveller Ket
stay.text: Hah! — hah! — thanks, friend! I'm gonna stay! I'm gonna remember this forever!

morning.speaker: Reveller Ket
morning.text: Oh — right, right, in the morning. I'll ask in the morning. Good idea. — Where's the music, I want to be near the music.

root.speaker: Old Wim
root.text: Mm. — Mind the broom, friend. This place needs sweeping every morning by someone. Same someone, mostly.

why.speaker: Old Wim
why.text: Festival leaves a lot. Petals. Cups. Names people drop and don't pick back up. — Someone has to. May as well be me.

bye.speaker: Old Wim
bye.text: Walk well. Mind the lanterns; the south one's tilting.

root.speaker: Hawker Pim
root.text: Seeds! Hot seeds! Five for five pebbles, friend, that's the festival rate — oh, you look new. Free taste, then! Just one. Don't tell the next traveller.

taste.speaker: Hawker Pim
taste.text: There y'are. Salt, fire, and a little bit of the morning. Hah! — walk well, friend, walk well!

later.speaker: Hawker Pim
later.text: Suit yourself! They'll be hot again tomorrow. Always are.

root.speaker: Costumer Vela
root.text: Mask, traveller? I make them in the colours of the morning. They wear well — for a day.

day.speaker: Costumer Vela
day.text: Mm. The dye runs by sunrise. Some of us don't notice. — Some of us notice and don't say.

taught.speaker: Costumer Vela
taught.text: An old woman in the corner, with a small book. She doesn't make masks. She told me what colour the morning is. I do the rest.

bye.speaker: Costumer Vela
bye.text: Walk well. The blue one suits you.

props.message:
TODAY ONLY —
The First Festival of the Year!
(Banners by Vela. Seeds by Pim.)

TODAY'S SCHEDULE
  First Drum   — morning
  Mask Parade  — noon
  Second Drum  — dusk
  Bonfire      — always

MISSING:
  A traveller in a green cloak.
  Probably just went home.
  We'll find them again tomorrow.

FRESHLY PAINTED —
  Today we remember _____.
  (paint left out for the next remembering.)

INN: BRALL'S
  Beds for one night.
  We have only ever needed one.

THE SOUTH BENCH
  Reserved for an old reader.
  Do not move.

HAWKER PIM'S STALL
  Seeds: 5 for 5 pebbles.
  Free taste for first-comers.
  Everyone is a first-comer.

VELA'S MASKS
  Worn for one day.
  No refunds. No reminders.

STAGE
  Step up, sing loud.
  The morning won't hold it against you.

DANCE FLOOR
  Mind the step down.
  Mind the step up.

BONFIRE PIT
  Tend nightly.
  Tend again in the morning.

WAY NORTH —
  Cold path, pale summit.
  Walk slow.

props.open_message: A small glass vessel. The old woman has left it for you. Walk well.
A festival favour. A small handful of pebbles.
A pouch of pebbles. Twelve, by the count of the morning.
A spark of warmth. The festival looks brighter.
A small key. The Drift hardly ever locks a door, but a key is a key.
A bone-and-wood boomerang. Press F to throw it; it returns on its own.

root.speaker: Festival-Goer Ralo
root.text: HO! HO ho ho! Walker! Look at THESE lanterns! Look at this one! It's painted to look like a moon-eyed rook! Hahaha — I love it, I LOVE it, who paints a rook on a lantern? Geniuses. Geniuses everywhere!

trade.speaker: Festival-Goer Ralo
trade.text:
WHAT — is that — by Lirien's bright breath, is that COMB-HONEY? In a JAR? Sealed in WAX?

*he clutches his chest, theatrical.*

Walker. Walker. I have been eating festival cakes for THREE DAYS. My tongue has forgotten what real sweetness is. I would trade my own NAME for that jar. — Wait, wait, I have something better. Here.

*he presses a painted mask into your hands — fox-faced, lacquer still tacky.*

My cousin painted it. She'd want it loved. Take it on. Take it on!

thanks.speaker: Festival-Goer Ralo
thanks.text:
*already prying the wax seal with his teeth.*

Go, go, walk well, the mask wants a road under it!

already.speaker: Festival-Goer Ralo
already.text: Ha! Still wearing my cousin's face? Good, GOOD! Show it the long road, walker, show it everything!

festival.speaker: Festival-Goer Ralo
festival.text: What's it FOR? It's for itself! That's the BEST kind! No anniversary, no harvest, no naming — somebody just hung the first lantern and the rest of us showed up. That's how the good ones start.

bye.speaker: Festival-Goer Ralo
bye.text: Walk WELL, walker! Walk loud! The road likes a noisy boot!

props.message:
Tucked in a corner of the carnival yard:

Old session lockfiles. Cleared at boot. Most of us don't ask.

root.speaker: Festival-goer
root.text:
*she is wearing yesterday's wreath, already wilting*

Yesterday's release? Forgotten. Tomorrow's? Will be. Today is the festival, walker, and the festival is the only thing.

yest.speaker: Festival-goer
yest.text:
*she thinks for a long second*

I do not remember. That is the point.
